engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz R Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz R Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Core 3.0L V6 diesel for R320 CDI; aluminium block, common-rail injection; timing chain issues defined replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: R 320 CDI, R 320 CDI 4MATIC, R 320 CDI Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch/guide failure (80k-120k miles)
Oil cooler seal leaks causing coolant contamination
Swirl flap mechanism failure in intake manifold
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642.950 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 188bhp OM642 for R280 CDI; shared architecture with higher-output variants; oil cooler seal failures common.
compatibleVariants: R 280 CDI, R 280 CDI 4MATIC, R 280 CDI Sport
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal leaks (labour-intensive repair)
EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642.870 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: BlueEfficiency 190bhp OM642 for R300 CDI; improved emissions but retained swirl flap and EGR cooler vulnerability.
compatibleVariants: R 300 CDI, R 300 CDI 4MATIC, R 300 CDI BlueEfficiency, R 300 CDI Sport
commonFailures: Swirl flap linkage failure causing power loss
EGR cooler internal cracking
Turbo bearing wear on sustained high-load use
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642.872 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 265bhp OM642 LS for R350 CDI; twin-turbo setup delivered performance but increased turbo actuator failure risk.
compatibleVariants: R 350 CDI, R 350 CDI 4MATIC, R 350 CDI Sport
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure (variable vane sticking)
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples
Oil cooler seal degradation
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 LS Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: AdBlue-equipped OM642 for R350 BlueTEC; reduced emissions but introduced SCR system complexity and injector clogging issues.
commonFailures: AdBlue injector clogging causing fault codes
NOx sensor failure triggering limp mode
DPF saturation on short-journey driving patterns
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 BlueTEC Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: 3.0L V6 petrol for R280/R300; aluminium construction, balance shaft design; pre-2007 sprocket wear a known terminal failure.
compatibleVariants: R 280, R 280 4MATIC, R 280 Sport, R 300, R 300 4MATIC, R 300 Sport
commonFailures: Balance shaft sprocket wear (pre-2007 engines, 70k-100k miles)
Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattle on cold start
Oil pump drive gear wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272.945 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Flagship 3.5L M272 for R350 petrol; 272bhp output; balance shaft sprocket wear on early units drove replacement enquiries.
commonFailures: Balance shaft sprocket wear (2005-2007 models, terminal failure)
Intake manifold swirl flap actuator failure
Oil leak from camshaft adjuster solenoids
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272.967 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early R500 5.0L SOHC V8; robust iron-block design but timing chain guide wear on high-mileage examples prompted replacement.
commonFailures: Timing chain guide plastic degradation (100k+ miles)
Valve cover gasket oil leaks
Secondary air injection pump failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113.970 Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.5L Petrol
entries.history: Facelift R500 5.5L DOHC V8; 382bhp output; cam adjuster and timing chain tensioner issues on post-2007 engines.
commonFailures: Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure causing rough idle
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples
Oil cooler seal leaks requiring engine removal

entries.title: 6.2L Petrol
entries.history: Hand-built 6.2L AMG V8 for R63; 510bhp naturally aspirated; head bolt and bearing failures on track-used examples.
commonFailures: Head bolt failure causing coolant/oil mixing (high-stress use)
Rod bearing wear on track-focused driving patterns
Oil consumption due to piston ring land design

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz R Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Mercedes Benz R Class reliability is heavily influenced by the OM642 V6 diesel and M272 V6 petrol families, both of which carry well-documented failure points that often dictate replacement decisions. Every rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class engine supplied through our network features upgraded internal components engineered to resolve these original design weaknesses. All units include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, giving you a clear, cost-effective alternative to uncertain partial repairs. The decision tool below compares realistic UK repair tiers against full replacement so you can make an informed, economically sound choice.

problems.group: Mercedes Benz R Class Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz R Class Timing Chain Failure - Why OM642 Diesel Engines Stretch
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz R Class R 320 CDI, R 350 CDI, R 300 CDI (2006-2012, OM642.950, OM642.870, OM642.872)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted tensioner loses hydraulic pressure over time, allowing the timing chain to stretch and prematurely wear plastic guides.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain tension, guide swap, and updated tensioner fit without engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles if caught before rattling escalates

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, bench rebuild with upgraded timing assembly, new seals, supply & fit installation

problems.recommendation: If you hear a metallic rattle on cold start between 900-1,200rpm, act immediately. A preventative chain and guide replacement is cost-effective on low-mileage examples. Once plastic guide fragments enter the oil sump or the tensioner fully collapses, a complete Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 rebuilt engine is the safer, more economical route.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz R Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz R Class Oil Cooler Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz R Class Oil Cooler Seal Failure - Why Coolant Contamination Occurs
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz R Class R 320 CDI, R 280 CDI (2006-2012, OM642 family)
problems.rootCause: Factory rubber sealing rings degrade under repeated thermal cycles, allowing pressurised engine oil to leak directly into the coolant matrix.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine disassembly to cooler housing, new gasket fit, coolant flush, and pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles; viable only if coolant is clean

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Cooler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete unit swap with factory-upgraded seal materials, new oil cooler pre-fitted, supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: Lifetime of vehicle when combined with regular servicing

problems.recommendation: Accessing the oil cooler on the Mercedes Benz R Class requires significant component removal. If the coolant shows milky contamination or the engine has run hot, a patch repair is a false economy. A rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 engine arrives with upgraded cooler seals already fitted, eliminating the most labour-intensive step.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz R Class oil cooler seal replacement & rebuilt engines

problems.group: Mercedes Benz R Class M272 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz R Class M272 Balance Shaft Failure - Why Early Petrol V6s Seize
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz R Class R 350, R 300 Petrol (2006-2008, M272.967, M272.945)
problems.rootCause: Pre-facelift M272 engines used a hardened steel balance shaft sprocket that wears prematurely, causing timing jump, valve-to-piston contact, and terminal failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine removal, crank disassembly, revised steel sprocket fit, timing realignment
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-70k miles; does not address existing bore wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M272 with Revised Design
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench rebuild using updated factory-spec sprockets, new bearings, upgraded gaskets, supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with full 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes Benz R Class M272 showing symptoms, the repair labour outweighs the part cost. We recommend bypassing partial rebuilds and opting for a complete rebuilt unit featuring the revised sprocket design. It guarantees mechanical integrity and preserves the vehicle's residual value.
problems.cta: Compare M272 balance shaft replacement vs rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz R Class Swirl Flap & EGR Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz R Class Swirl Flap & EGR Failure - Why Diesel Power Drops
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz R Class R 350 CDI, R 320 CDI, R 350 BlueTEC (2006-2012, OM642 variants)
problems.rootCause: Heavy carbon deposits cause swirl flap shafts to seize or snap, while the EGR cooler housing cracks under thermal stress, mixing exhaust gases into the cooling system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Intake manifold removal, carbon decarbonisation, swirl flap replacement, new EGR cooler fit
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles; temporary fix on high-soot engines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Diesel with Updated Intake
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine exchange with revised intake routing, upgraded cooler, new injectors, supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with resolved emissions & boost delivery

problems.recommendation: Component replacement works if caught early and driving patterns include regular motorway use. However, if broken flap fragments enter cylinders or the EGR cooler has contaminated the coolant circuit, a full Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 rebuilt engine is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class swirl flap repair vs full engine replacement quotes

problems.group: Mercedes Benz R Class Turbocharger Actuator Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz R Class Turbocharger Actuator Failure - Why Boost Pressure Drops
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz R Class R 350 CDI, R 350 BlueTEC (2009-2012, OM642 LS)
problems.rootCause: Variable vane geometry seizes from soot accumulation and heat cycling, triggering electronic actuator faults, reduced turbo response, and persistent limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator software reset, vane cleaning, new position sensor, boost pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: 20k-40k miles; fails if internal vanes are pitted

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete unit swap featuring remanufactured turbo with revised actuator, new wastegate, supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with restored factory torque curve and 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: Cleaning and calibration rarely fix advanced Mercedes Benz R Class turbo failures. We recommend pairing turbo replacement with a rebuilt engine if compression or oil pressure is marginal. This approach eliminates recurring limp mode codes and delivers consistent towing performance.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz R Class turbo actuator & rebuilt engine supply

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz R Class
finalCta.paragraph: Navigating Mercedes Benz R Class engine problems requires balancing repair costs against the vehicle's current market value. Main dealer quotes frequently exceed the car's worth, especially when labour-intensive repairs like chain tensioners, oil cooler seals, or balance shaft replacements are required. Our network connects you directly with vetted UK Mercedes Benz R Class engine specialists who provide upgraded rebuilt units, transparent supply & fit pricing, and nationwide delivery. Compare verified quotes today and secure your engine with a guaranteed 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, avoiding the main dealer premium without compromising on precision engineering.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz R Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz R Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

groups.title: Mercedes Benz R Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre OM642 V6 diesel was the sole compression-ignition powerplant offered in the UK R-Class range from launch through to the final facelift. It formed the backbone of the lineup, balancing strong low-end torque for a heavy seven-seat MPV with relatively refined NVH characteristics. Despite its robust construction, prolonged high-load usage and missed service intervals expose critical weaknesses in the timing and lubrication systems.
commonFailurePoints: oil cooler seal degradation leading to coolant contamination
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz R Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz R Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced to broaden the appeal of the R-Class in the UK market, this naturally aspirated V6 petrol unit provided a smoother, quieter alternative to the dominant diesel offerings. It features an aluminium block, variable valve timing, and a single overhead cam design that prioritises refinement over outright performance. Pre-facelift iterations are particularly noted for a specific balance shaft design flaw that demands proactive replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Hardened steel balance shaft sprocket wear causing timing misalignment
terminal engine damage on pre-2007 units
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Serving as the volume petrol choice for the R 350 variant, the larger displacement V6 delivers stronger mid-range torque and improved acceleration for fully laden family touring. It shares the core architecture of the 3.0-litre unit but benefits from revised intake runners and an updated engine management calibration. Later facelift models incorporate minor internal revisions that improve long-term oil consumption control.
commonFailurePoints: Intake manifold swirl flap actuator failure
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The early R 500 generation utilised this SOHC V8, a heavily revised version of Mercedes’ long-running iron-block architecture known for exceptional durability under sustained load. It was paired with the 4MATIC system as standard, providing effortless overtaking power and towing capability for large family MPVs. Its simple, robust design avoids the complexity of modern direct injection, making it highly sought after by specialist rebuilders.
commonFailurePoints: valve cover gasket oil seepage on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Facelifted R 500 models transitioned to this DOHC 32-valve V8, which introduced variable valve timing and improved efficiency while significantly increasing power output. The unit features a lighter aluminium block and advanced engine management to meet stricter emissions standards without sacrificing the effortless refinement expected from a flagship Mercedes MPV. Despite its advanced engineering, oil system maintenance remains critical to longevity.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattling
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class 5.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz R Class 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Hand-assembled at the Mercedes-AMG facility in Affalterbach, this high-revving naturally aspirated V8 was exclusively fitted to the rare R 63 AMG flagship variant. It represents the pinnacle of the R-Class powertrain lineup, delivering supercar-rivaling acceleration in a seven-seat luxury tourer format. The engine is built to extreme tolerances with forged internals, but track-focused abuse often precipitates premature bearing and head gasket degradation.
commonFailurePoints: Head bolt failure under high thermal stress
rod bearing wear from aggressive driving patterns
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz R Class 6.2L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz R Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline tracks every engine introduction, facelift update, and fuel system change across the W251/V251 generation so you can instantly identify your unit and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The debut year introduced the heavy seven-seat luxury MPV platform with two V6 families and a robust early V8, immediately attracting high-mileage family and commercial buyers.
keyChanges: UK market launch of the W251/V251 luxury MPV chassis
Introduction of OM642.950 (diesel) and M272.967 (petrol) V6 families
Early R 500 fitted with M113.970 SOHC V8 for maximum towing capacity
Standard 4MATIC all-wheel drive paired with 5G/7G-Tronic transmissions
knownFor: Heavy build quality and exceptional motorway cruising refinement
Pre-2007 M272 balance shaft sprocket wear prone to terminal failure
OM642 rear timing chain tensioner design establishes long-term replacement pattern
M113 V8 delivers legendary reliability but suffers guide degradation at high mileage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006 Mercedes Benz R Class replacement engines
years.ticker: 2006 Mercedes Benz R 320 CDI engine replacement • 2006 R 350 M272 balance shaft failure • 2006 R 500 V8 engine quote
enquiries: 2006 Mercedes Benz R 320 CDI engine replacement

years.description: This period marked the arrival of the track-derived R 63 AMG and the transition of the R 500 to the newer M273 architecture, shifting the lineup toward higher output and specialist demand.
keyChanges: Launch of the hand-built R 63 AMG featuring the M156 6.2L naturally aspirated V8
R 500 transitions from M113 to the DOHC M273.963 5.5L V8
Minor software calibration updates to OM642 emissions mapping
Introduction of premium interior trim packages for UK market
knownFor: R 63 AMG delivers supercar-rivaling acceleration in a luxury family hauler format
M273 V8 improves efficiency but introduces camshaft adjuster solenoid leak risks
High-performance use accelerates rod bearing wear on M156 units
2007-2008 OM642 models remain highly susceptible to timing chain guide fragmentation
years.cta: Compare 2007 to 2008 Mercedes Benz R Class engine costs
years.ticker: 2007 Mercedes Benz R 63 AMG engine price • 2008 R 320 CDI timing chain failure • 2007 R 500 M273 engine replacement
enquiries: 2007 Mercedes Benz R 63 AMG engine price

years.description: The 2009 model year brought comprehensive styling revisions, updated engine management, and the crucial BlueEfficiency package aimed at reducing emissions and improving economy.
keyChanges: Full exterior and interior facelift with revised suspension tuning
Introduction of OM642.872 (R 300 CDI) and OM642 LS (R 350 CDI) variants
M272 V6 updated with revised intake manifolds and improved oil consumption control
Implementation of BlueEfficiency start-stop and low-rolling-resistance tyres
knownFor: Facelift significantly improved cabin noise insulation and steering response
OM642.872 suffers from swirl flap actuator failure and EGR cooler cracking
High-output OM642 LS twin-turbo setup exposes actuator and turbo bearing weaknesses
Post-2009 M272 engines largely resolve the earlier balance shaft terminal defect

years.ticker: 2009 Mercedes Benz R 300 CDI engine replacement • 2009 R 350 CDI OM642 LS quote • 2009 R 350 petrol M272 price
enquiries: 2009 Mercedes Benz R 300 CDI engine replacement

years.description: The final UK production years focused on stringent Euro 5 compliance, integrating AdBlue SCR technology into the diesel lineup before the MPV was withdrawn from British showrooms.
keyChanges: Introduction of the OM642 BlueTEC variant with selective catalytic reduction
Revised DPF regeneration strategies and exhaust gas temperature sensors
Transmission software updates for smoother 7G-Tronic Plus shifting
Final UK model year concludes in 2012 (continued in China as LWB until 2017)
knownFor: BlueTEC models face AdBlue injector clogging and NOx sensor failures on high mileage
DPF blockage remains a critical issue for short-journey UK urban driving patterns
Late-model W251 chassis represents the most refined suspension calibration of the generation
Withdrawal from UK market creates niche replacement demand with limited OEM stock

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz R Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 diesel engine typically costs £2,800-£4,600 supply-only in the UK, while petrol M272 units range £2,400-£4,200. Adding supply-and-fit installation increases total cost by £800-£1,500 depending on workshop location. Prices vary by engine code, variant specification, and supplier. Always confirm warranty terms and component upgrades before accepting any quote.
keyPoints: OM642.950 diesel engines for R320 CDI average £2,800-£4,600 rebuilt supply-only
M272.967 petrol engines for R350 typically cost £2,500-£4,500 reconditioned
Supply-and-fit labour adds £800-£1,500 depending on workshop location and 4MATIC complexity
Used engines start around £1,500 but carry higher risk without comprehensive warranty
Always request written warranty confirmation covering parts and labour for minimum 12 months
items.cta: Compare verified Mercedes Benz R Class engine quotes from UK specialists now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Mercedes Benz R Class?
items.answer: Replacing a Mercedes Benz R Class engine is worth it if the chassis, transmission and interior remain in good condition. With vehicle values at £3,000-£8,000 and rebuilt OM642 engines at £2,800-£4,600, the decision hinges on overall vehicle health and your intended ownership period.
keyPoints: Mercedes Benz R Class values range £3,000-£8,000 depending on year, mileage and specification
Rebuilt OM642 diesel engines cost £2,800-£4,600 supply-only from UK specialists
Factor in £800-£1,500 supply-and-fit labour when calculating total replacement investment
A quality rebuilt engine with warranty can extend vehicle life by 50,000+ miles
Consider scrappage if bodywork, suspension or transmission also need major investment
items.cta: Get a tailored Mercedes Benz R Class engine replacement quote based on your variant

items.question: What causes the R320 CDI timing chain to fail on the OM642 engine?
items.answer: The Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 timing chain fails due to rear-mounted tensioner wear allowing chain stretch, which damages plastic guides. This typically occurs between 80,000-120,000 miles on R320 CDI and R350 CDI variants if oil service intervals are extended or missed.
keyPoints: OM642 rear timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure after 80,000 miles on R Class
Plastic guide fragments from chain stretch can enter oil sump causing catastrophic secondary damage
Missed oil changes or using incorrect specification oil accelerates OM642 timing component wear
Cold-start rattling between 900-1,200rpm is the key early warning symptom for R Class owners
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz R Class before failure escalates

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz R Class engine is most reliable - diesel or petrol?
items.answer: Post-2009 Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 diesel engines are generally more reliable than pre-2007 M272 petrol units, which suffer balance shaft sprocket wear. However, all OM642 variants require strict oil service adherence to avoid timing chain and oil cooler seal issues common on the R Class platform.
keyPoints: Pre-2007 M272 petrol engines in R350 suffer terminal balance shaft sprocket wear around 70,000 miles
Post-2009 OM642 diesel engines benefit from revised tensioners reducing timing chain failure risk on R Class
OM642 oil cooler seal leaks remain a labour-intensive repair regardless of diesel or petrol R Class variant
R500 V8 M113 and M273 units offer robust reliability but command premium replacement costs for Mercedes Benz R Class
items.cta: Find the right rebuilt engine for your Mercedes Benz R Class variant and budget today

items.question: How long will a rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 engine with upgraded timing components and seals can reliably deliver 100,000+ miles when maintained to manufacturer specifications. The critical factor is using a specialist who addresses the original design weaknesses during the rebuild process.
keyPoints: Quality OM642 rebuilds include upgraded tensioners, guides and oil cooler seals for Mercedes Benz R Class
Adhering to 10,000-mile oil change intervals using MB 229.51 specification oil extends rebuilt engine lifespan
Rebuilt OM642 engines from EngineMarket specialists carry minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty coverage
Avoid short-journey driving patterns that accelerate DPF and EGR system wear on rebuilt R Class diesel engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 rebuilt engines with comprehensive warranty coverage

items.question: Can I use a used Mercedes Benz R Class engine instead of reconditioned?
items.answer: Yes, a used Mercedes Benz R Class engine can be a budget option at £1,500-£2,800, but carries higher risk without comprehensive testing or warranty. Reconditioned OM642 units at £2,800-£4,600 include upgraded components addressing known R Class failure points and come with 12-month warranty protection.
keyPoints: Used OM642 engines for Mercedes Benz R Class typically cost £1,500-£2,800 supply-only from UK breakers
Always request compression test results and service history documentation before purchasing a used R Class engine
Reconditioned engines include new gaskets, seals and upgraded timing components resolving original Mercedes Benz R Class weaknesses
Used engines rarely include warranty coverage beyond 30-90 days versus 12 months on reconditioned R Class units
items.cta: Compare used versus reconditioned Mercedes Benz R Class engine options with warranty details included

items.question: Does the Mercedes Benz R Class engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Every Mercedes Benz R Class engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour. Always confirm the exact warranty terms with your chosen specialist, as coverage can vary between suppliers for OM642 and M272 rebuilt units.
keyPoints: EngineMarket network specialists provide minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on all Mercedes Benz R Class rebuilt engines
Warranty coverage typically includes parts and labour for engine-related failures during the protection period
Always request written warranty documentation specifying inclusions, exclusions and claim procedures for your R Class engine
Supply-and-fit installations often extend warranty coverage to include fitting labour unlike supply-only engine purchases
items.cta: Find Mercedes Benz R Class engine suppliers offering comprehensive 12-month warranty protection today

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes Benz R Class engine code?
items.answer: Your Mercedes Benz R Class engine code appears on the engine block stamping, vehicle V5C registration document, or by entering your registration on the EngineMarket lookup tool. Common codes include OM642.950 for R320 CDI and M272.967 for R350 petrol variants.
keyPoints: Mercedes Benz R Class engine codes are stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head
The V5C registration document lists your engine code in section D.2 for Mercedes Benz R Class vehicles
Enter your registration on EngineMarket to instantly identify your R Class engine code and compatible replacements
OM642 variants include .950, .870, .872 and LS suffixes denoting power output for different R Class models
items.cta: Enter your Mercedes Benz R Class registration to identify your exact engine code and replacement options

items.question: Is the Mercedes Benz R Class engine compatible with ML Class or M-Class?
items.answer: The Mercedes Benz R Class OM642 and M272 engines share architecture with contemporary ML-Class and M-Class models, but mounting points, wiring looms and ECU calibration differ. Never assume cross-model compatibility - always verify your specific Mercedes Benz R Class engine code against donor vehicle specifications.
keyPoints: OM642 diesel engines in Mercedes Benz R Class share core design with ML320 CDI and M-Class W164 variants
Engine mounting brackets, wiring harnesses and ECU software differ between Mercedes Benz R Class and ML-Class applications
Using an ML-Class donor engine in an R Class requires specialist adaptation of mounts and electronics
Always match engine code suffixes precisely when sourcing replacement units for Mercedes Benz R Class W251 chassis
